title: For Loops Over Text
summary: Loop through a string one character at a time.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to For Loops
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, a for loop allows you to iterate over a sequence, such as a string. This means you can access each character in the string one by one. For example, if you have the string `"hello"`, you can loop through it to print each character:

```python
for char in "hello":
    print(char)
```

This will output:
```
h
e
l
l
o
```

**Try it yourself:** Write a for loop to print each character in the string `"Python"`.

sketchBlocks.title: Counting Characters in a String
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can also use a for loop to count the number of characters in a string. For example:

```python
count = 0
for char in "hello":
    count += 1
print(count)
```

This will output `5`, which is the number of characters in the string `"hello"`.

**Your turn:** Modify the code to count the characters in the string `"programming"`.

quizDraft.title: Print Each Character
quizDraft.prompt: Write a for loop that prints each character of the string `"Hello, World!"`.
quizDraft.starterCode:
for char in "Hello, World!":
    # Your code here

quizDraft.title: Complete the Loop
quizDraft.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete the for loop that counts characters in a string.

```python
count = 0
for char in "______":
    count += 1
print(count)
```
quizDraft.template:
count = 0
for char in "{blank}":
    count += 1
print(count)

quizDraft.title: For Loop Output
quizDraft.prompt:
What will the following code output?

```python
for char in "abc":
    print(char)
```
